exemplar.title: How do the shape and dimensions of reflective surfaces inside a kaleidoscope affect the patterns formed and can mathematical modelling be utilised to enhance the visual appeal of these patterns?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do the shape and dimensions of reflective surfaces inside a kaleidoscope affect the patterns formed and can mathematical modelling be utilised to enhance the visual appeal of these patterns?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear division into introduction, methodology, results and conclusion providing logical progression
Detailed table of contents, numbered headings and consistent diagram labels enhancing navigation
Concise title and well-articulated research question orienting the reader immediately
weaknesses: Assessor comments embedded in the text interrupt the flow
Repeated headings and restated title create redundancy
Lengthy literature review and occasional placeholder text reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates good coherence and clear organization with only minor digressions and redundancies. Two strands are in Band 3 (coherence and organization) and one in Band 2 (conciseness), supporting a Band 3 overall score (3/4).

strengths: Consistent and mostly accurate use of mathematical notation (degrees, logarithms, variables)
Highly effective use of multiple representations (formulae, tables, graphs, GeoGebra screenshots)
Logical sequencing of arguments supported by clear captions and diagrams
weaknesses: Occasional minor notational errors (e.g. incorrect denominator in a fraction)
Lack of formal definitions in the mathematical modelling section
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite isolated slips, the student employs mathematical language and multiple representations effectively with clear communication throughout. This performance aligns with Band 4 (4/4) for Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Thorough social survey design and clear primary data-gathering initiative
Personal rationale linking past experience to research motivation
Creative, step-by-step instructions for constructing a DIY kaleidoscope model
weaknesses: Some methodological steps mirror standard textbook procedures
Mathematical depth of the creative element remains moderate
Innovation is notable but not consistently pushed beyond routine exploration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows an outstanding personal stampâcombining survey design, fractal analysis and physical modelingâthat goes beyond routine work. This merits the top band for Personal Engagement (3/3).

strengths: Meaningful discussion of model limitations, mirror misalignment and subjectivity in survey results
Thoughtful comparison of results against expectations and interpretation of discrepancies
Clear concluding evaluation with suggested future improvements
weaknesses: Survey data lack basic statistical commentary (mean, mode, trends)
Some limitations end abruptly without full explanation
Regression analysis omits fit metrics (RÂ², correlation) for deeper insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and basic critical analysis throughout but stops short of the deep, comprehensive evaluation characteristic of the top band. The work thus aligns with Band 2 for Reflection (2/3).

strengths: Correct application of the angleâofâreflection formula and clear derivation of n=360/Î¸
Effective use of boxâcounting methods for fractal dimension
Neat linear regression analysis connecting data to aesthetic outcomes
weaknesses: Justification for halving the rotation angle is not fully explained
Typographical inconsistencies in boxâcount data (e.g. â53/48â)
Some mathematical techniques remain at a basic SL level without deeper extensions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies relevant mathematics appropriately and at the expected course level with generally good understanding and only minor errors. This performance fits Band 4 (4/6) for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are usually presented in a clear sequence and supported by diagrams or data; readers can follow how conclusions are reached.  Isolated notational errors and some abrupt transitions limit absolute clarity, keeping the work in the good band.

Derivations are generally correct and the student shows solid understanding, yet there are noticeable inaccuracies (e.g. dividing by 2 in degrees, inconsistent box counts), so the work demonstrates good rather than thorough correctness.

The student employs formulae, tables, graphs, GeoGebra screenshots and photographs of physical models.  These serve the communication task effectively, but captions and links between representations are sometimes brief, so the use is good rather than outstanding.

The exploration is divided into clear sections (introduction, methodology, results, conclusion) that follow one another logically; the reader can track how the investigation develops from the research question to the findings.  Minor digressions and mid-text teacher comments occasionally interrupt the flow, so the logical thread is good but not exemplary.

Using a Hausdorff dimension to quantify pattern appeal and combining it with a Likert survey displays inventiveness and initiative beyond a routine treatment, yet the mathematical depth of the creative element remains moderate.

Results are compared against expectations (e.g. survey outcome versus fractal dimension), and the student interprets why discrepancies arise.  The analysis is thoughtful but not deeply probing or mathematically rigorous.

A concluding section summarises findings and outlines several limitations and future improvements.  While the evaluation is clear, it lacks the comprehensive, forward-looking insight characteristic of the top band.

The write-up includes discussions of mirror misalignment, survey subjectivity and model limitations, showing meaningful reflection.  Reflection is woven through major sections, but the depth stops short of continuous critical commentary.

Degrees, logarithms and variables are generally written correctly and equations are set out clearly.  Some slips (e.g. $\frac{120^{\circ}}{2^{\circ}}$) appear, so usage is mostly correct rather than fully consistent.

Building DIY models, photographing interim stages and linking them to GeoGebra simulations give the exploration a recognisable personal stamp.  The approach is clearly student-directed but not highly innovative.

Most material relates directly to the research question, yet there is noticeable repetition (e.g. the research question is restated many times) and a lengthy, only loosely relevant literature review.  Overall relevance is acceptable but conciseness is only moderate.

Geometry of reflections, basic trigonometry ($n=360/\theta$) and box-counting fractal dimension are all relevant to the question and applied appropriately.  The mathematical content is clearly connected, though rather elementary for some parts, positioning it in the appropriate band.

The student chooses an unusual context, designs a survey, performs fractal analysis and constructs a physical kaleidoscope, showing clear independent decision-making.  Nonetheless, many steps closely mirror textbook optics and standard box-counting procedures, so independence is significant but not outstanding.

A detailed table of contents, numbered headings and sub-headings, and consistent labelling of diagrams demonstrate clear organisation.  However, the mixture of student text and assessor remarks, plus some duplicated headings, prevents it reaching the faultless structure expected for the top band.

Most techniques lie within or just above the SL syllabus (angle of rotation, symmetry groups, logarithms).  The level matches course expectations but lacks the breadth or sophistication needed for a higher band.

annotations.comment: The title is restated here, creating redundancy. Consider removing duplicate headings for conciseness and to avoid repetition.
The detailed table of contents demonstrates clear organisation, enabling the reader to navigate the exploration effectively.
The contents list is highly detailed but could be streamlined by consolidating sub-headings that are too granular, improving overall conciseness.
The introduction paragraph effectively contextualises kaleidoscopes in both historical and scientific terms, engaging the reader.
The introduction section is rather verbose and could be more concise. Trim auxiliary details to sharpen the focus on key concepts.
The studentâs rationale section conveys genuine personal engagement by linking past experiences to the explorationâs motivation.
The aim is detailed and connects mathematical modelling with aesthetic outcomes, bridging theory and practical application well.
The mathematical modelling section outlines methods but lacks definitions for specific transformations. Include formal definitions for clarity.
The title is concise and clearly indicates the explorationâs focus, immediately orienting the reader to the subject matter.
The research question is well articulated, specifying variables and objectives, which provides clear direction for the investigation.
Assessor comments remain embedded in the student text, interrupting the logical flow. Remove or relocate feedback to a separate section to maintain coherence.
Header clearly denotes the course and internal assessment, reinforcing the documentâs academic context.
Listing independent and dependent variables clearly defines the studyâs scope and facilitates later analysis of results.
Consider specifying control variables or constants to strengthen experimental design and isolate the effect of mirror geometry.
The literature review presents relevant sources but often drifts into narrative. Strengthen critical reflection by linking each reference explicitly to the research question.
Citation formats vary between web links and journal style, reducing consistency. Adopt one citation style throughout for professional coherence.
The formula $$n=\frac{360^{\circ}}{\theta}$$ is correctly stated, demonstrating appropriate use of mathematical notation.
The subsequent step dividing by 2 to compute rotation per reflection lacks justification. Explain why the angle is halved to avoid confusion.
The expression $$\frac{120^{\circ}}{2^{\circ}}$$ contains an incorrect divisor in the denominator. It should reflect halving correctly, not â2Â°.â
The diagram illustrating reflections for 120Â° rotation is clear and effectively supports the textual explanation.
Social survey methodology is thoroughly described, evidencing significant personal initiative in gathering primary data.
The survey results are presented without statistical commentary or interpretation. Add brief analysis (mean, mode, trends) to enhance depth of reflection.
The survey chart is well inserted and annotated, aiding reader comprehension of respondent preferences.
Placeholder text (ââ¢ Copy chartâ) remains in the results section and should be removed to maintain professional presentation.
Box-counting data are tabulated clearly, facilitating transparent computation of fractal dimensions.
Table 5 lists â53/48â for N(S), which appears as a typographical error. Verify and replace with a single numeric count for accuracy.
The linear regression plot is neatly produced, and the caption clarifies its purpose in deriving fractal dimensions.
Regression equations omit RÂ² or correlation values, which would indicate fit quality. Include these metrics for more rigorous analysis.
Stepwise instructions for constructing the kaleidoscope display significant creativity and independent thinking.
Lists are deeply nested and lengthy; consider flattening or numbering bullet points to improve readability.
The conclusion summarises the investigation effectively and answers the research question with clear links to mathematical findings.
Some limitations end mid-sentence, leaving points unfinished. Expand on these to ensure each limitation is fully discussed.
The bibliography covers diverse sources, from software documentation to academic journals, supporting the explorationâs credibility.
Citation styles vary across entries (APA, web, list items). Consistent formatting is needed to meet scholarly presentation standards.
